---
title: "Introduction"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Introduction}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r setup}
library(preflightnma)
```

We designed the package to consume the exact same "tidy" long-format data structure that multinma uses to define a network (`set_agd_arm()`, `set_agd_contrast()`, `combine_network()`).

The workflow is:

* Format data using `multinma::combine_network()`.

* Run Preflight: Pass the network object to `nma_preflight(network)`.

* Output: A Quarto HTML report containing your structural checks, covariate dot plots.

* Go/No-Go Decision: The analyst reviews the report. If the network is transitive and the priors are sensible, they proceed.

* Run Model using `multinma::nma()`.
